Align or add scripts/context_pack_gate.py if Write-gate enforcement is required

queries: When does Cache-Augmented Generation beat RAG for bounded knowledge?
hops.findingSummary: Peer-reviewed short paper argues CAG (preload/cache bounded corpora in long context) can beat classic RAG on latency and retrieval-error axes for constrained knowledge tasks; dynamic corpora still favor retrieval.

verificationEvidence.note: Primary PDF on arXiv; ACM WWW companion listing exists—verify venue details before citing as 'WWW full paper'.

queries: arXiv 2412.15605 Don't Do RAG Cache-Augmented Generation
hops.findingSummary: Same arXiv id surfaces on ADS abstract index and Hugging Face papers hub; use arXiv PDF as canonical.

verificationEvidence.note: Hop 1b lab/proceeding-style anchor; not reproduced locally.

queries: Model Context Protocol tools/list tools/call JSON-RPC 2025-11-25
hops.findingSummary: ASS-ADE MCP exposes context_memory_store/query with extended optional min_score on query; contract lives in server tool schemas.

verificationEvidence.note: Repo is authority for tool inputSchema; compare to https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/server/tools when changing shapes.

risksAndGaps: context_pack_gate.py referenced by Cursor command / AGENTS may be absent—Writes gated by hook could be no-ops or fail in CI until added.
Hashing embeddings are not neural cosine semantics; min_score defaults must stay conservative or env-driven.
setup_cursor_atomadic.py lives under ~/.cursor/atomadic—not versioned in !ass-ade; drift only visible after manual sync.
